title: Deborah Scott on Her Historic Oscar Nomination for Avatar: Fire and Ash
text: Costume designer Deborah Scott received her first-ever Oscar nomination for digitally rendered costumes in James Cameron's Avatar: Fire and Ash a historic first for performance-capture films. Scott emphasized her gratitude to the Academy, stating: I have to give kudos to my Academy branch for acknowledging the fact that this is outside the norm and embracing this innovative approach to costume design. Scott and her team created physical costumes that were then transformed into virtual versions by Wėta Workshop and Wėta FX digital artists. The process involved collaboration and iteration, with Scott providing feedback on designs already produced digitally. When discussing standout pieces, Scott highlighted two battle costumes one worn by villain Varang played by Oona Chaplin and another by protagonist Neytiri played by Zoe Saldana. She described designing Varang's outfit as capturing the journey of her character and being the full expression of the character's arc. Scott noted that Neytiri's battle costume was particularly challenging, explaining she searched for that design until finding the right narrative and emotional statement the costume needed to convey. Scott sees this nomination as recognition that costume design can exist in a lot of different formats, not just the traditional way we think of it, reflecting evolving possibilities in the field.
